hypothesis: If universal machine-learning interatomic potentials trained on broad molecular datasets are applied to metal–organic frameworks and related nanoporous materials, then they will reproduce DFT (PBE-D3(BJ)) reference optimized geometries, MD stability (volume evolution), bulk modulus, and 300 K heat capacities within small application-relevant errors because the learned equivariant architectures capture the essential interatomic and dispersion interactions.
